data.title: User Loses Over $68,000 in Transfer Record Pollution Attack
data.description: According to Foresight News, Scam Sniffer has detected a transfer record pollution attack that resulted in a user losing more than $68,000. The attacker employed a tactic involving the sending of fake transfers with similar addresses, causing the fraudulent address to appear in the user's transaction history. This led the user to mistakenly transfer funds to the attacker's address.

data.title: Cardex: Working with Abstract team to track stolen funds
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Abstract previously disclosed that the ecological application Cardex suffered a security vulnerability attack. After completing the initial security audit, its team accidentally exposed the private key of the session signer on the front end of its website. In response, Cardex issued a statement on the X platform confirming that it had been attacked related to the leakage of session keys. It is currently working with the Abstract team to track the flow and recovery of the stolen funds, and will disclose the details of the incident later.

data.title: SEC voluntarily withdraws appeal of DeFi rulemaking
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The U.S. SEC voluntarily withdrew its appeal of a ruling on Wednesday that prevented the agency from expanding the scope of existing securities laws to cover DeFi users and projects.
The SEC expressed its desire to "voluntarily withdraw this appeal" in a four-page motion filed with the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit, and the move was not opposed.
Last November, a federal judge in Texas ruled that the SEC's expansion of the legal definition of "dealer" exceeded the regulator's authority. The judge believed that the amendment to the SEC's dealer rules was illegal because it conflated DeFi traders with financial brokers.
Kristin Smith, CEO of the Blockchain Association, said in a statement that the SEC's voluntary withdrawal of the appeal was a major victory for the digital asset industry. "We first filed a lawsuit against the SEC to challenge the agency's illegal power grab, which attempted to unilaterally redefine the boundaries of its statutory powers. With the agency's new leadership leading to today's final dismissal, we look forward to a productive dialogue between the industry and the SEC." (Decrypt)

data.title: Victim Loses Over $68,000 Due To Incorrect CEX Deposit Address
data.description: According to Odaily, a victim recently lost $68,194 after mistakenly copying an incorrect centralized exchange (CEX) deposit address from a contaminated transaction record. The incident was detected by Scam Sniffer 27 minutes prior to the report.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Launches Open Intents Framework to Enhance Interoperability
data.description: According to Odaily, the Ethereum Foundation has introduced the Open Intents Framework, a modular open-source framework designed to build and deploy intent-based product experiences. This initiative involves collaboration with over 30 teams from the Ethereum ecosystem to accelerate interoperability. The framework's modular stack includes key components such as resolution and settlement, allowing developers the flexibility to mix and match parts that best suit their specific needs without being restricted to a single vendor. The framework is now live and supported by Ethereum Layer 2 solutions, wallets, and infrastructure providers, with additional features expected to be released soon.

data.title: Yesterday, Fidelity FBTC had a net outflow of $48.4 million and FETH had a net inflow of $24.5 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Farside Investors data, Fidelity FBTC had a net outflow of US$48.4 million yesterday, and FETH had a net inflow of US$24.5 million.

data.title: White House official: Trump will sign executive order to end federal overreach in regulation and law enforcement
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: White House officials said that US President Trump will sign an executive order to end excessive federal intervention in regulation and law enforcement and restore the separation of powers stipulated in the Constitution. (Jinshi)

data.title: Musk: Grok will launch standalone apps for MacOS and Windows
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Musk posted on the X platform that Grok will also provide services as a standalone application for MacOS and Windows.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Aims To Abolish IRS, Says Commerce Secretary
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick stated that U.S. President Donald Trump aims to abolish the Internal Revenue Service (IRS).

data.title: Ethereum Foundation launches Open Intents Framework to promote cross-chain interoperability
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The Ethereum Foundation announced the launch of the Open Intents Framework, a modular open source framework for building and deploying intent product experiences, accelerating interoperability by more than 30 teams from the Ethereum ecosystem.
By modularizing key components of the stack (such as resolution and settlement), the framework gives developers the flexibility to mix and match the parts that best suit their specific needs without being restricted to a single vendor.
The framework is now live and supported by Ethereum L2, wallet and infrastructure providers. More features are coming soon.
